title: carex astricta
intro: Carex astricta, also known as bottlebrush sedge, is a perennial plant species within the sedge family (Cyperaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions and wounds due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Carex astricta include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. The plant is often prepared as a herbal tea or infusion by steeping the dried leaves and rhizomes in hot water, or used in topical applications such as salves and creams.
